checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all governance proposals and votes are validated through strong cryptographic signature checks to prevent spoofing and unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System's signature verification mechanism has a flaw.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions, secure key management by users, and regular audits of Governance System contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating signature verification process; code reviews confirming signature validation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure session management and credential hygiene for BeraHub frontend
checklist_items.description: Implement strong session controls and educate users to prevent session hijacking or impersonation attacks during delegation and token management activities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend's session management may be vulnerable to hijacking.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use strong cryptographic signatures for all delegation transactions, enforce secure session management, user education, and transaction verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Session logs; user awareness training materials; code reviews of session handling.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all onboarding and administrative access to Reward Vaults is protected with multisignature controls and timelocks
checklist_items.description: Apply multi-factor authentication and enforce governance-controlled changes to incentive configurations to prevent unauthorized tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault's admin functions may contain access control vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement timelocks and multisignature requirements for admin actions, perform regular access audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs; smart contract audit reports; governance policy documentation.

checklist_items.title: Require all Non-Owner Signers to verify transaction parameters before signing multisig cancellation transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify the details (target proposal, cancellation reason) of each transaction before signing to prevent tampering and misexecution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Signers may not verify transaction parameters for multisig cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement procedural controls that require signers to verify all transaction details thoroughly before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction records; sign-off logs; procedural checklists.

checklist_items.title: Ensure off-chain and on-chain validation of reward configuration data for PoL mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Implement strict validation to reject malformed or malicious reward allocation data before it influences $BGT emission and delegation behavior.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward configuration data may be tampered with if not validated properly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply strict validation, including schema checks and on-chain invariant enforcement, for reward configuration inputs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test logs demonstrating validation failure for invalid inputs; smart contract audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit smart contracts involved in asset bridging for vulnerabilities and backdoors
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits of the bridge smart contracts and off-chain components to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could allow tampering or theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Berachain Bridge contracts may contain exploitable vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Schedule periodic formal audits and code reviews by reputable security firms; implement bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports; security test results; bug bounty submission logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals and votes submitted to the governance system are validated using strong cryptographic signatures from legitimate $BGT holders.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The signature verification mechanism within the Governance System is flawed or bypassable, allowing spoofed or tampered proposals and votes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions. Use verified key management practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs demonstrating successful signature verification process, audit reports of verification logic.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls and role management for Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized entities (e.g., DAO governance, multi-signature wallets) can modify incentive parameters within Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contracts contain vulnerabilities or weak access control mechanisms that can be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous access control, role-based permissions, and multisignature requirements for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control audit logs, code review reports, and role assignment records.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication and secure key management for validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Ensure validators use hardware wallets or secure management practices to protect their private keys used for signing consensus and staking transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored insecurely or are vulnerable to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules or multi-signature setups, and enforce secure operational procedures for key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and hardware wallet audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Deploy anti-phishing measures and user education for interacting with dApps
checklist_items.description: Educate users to verify URLs and contract addresses, and implement domain monitoring to prevent engagement with malicious dApp sites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived into interacting with phishing sites.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement domain verification, user training, and official communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User awareness materials, phishing site monitoring reports.

checklist_items.title: Secure frontend interfaces with integrity checks and verification techniques
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all user-facing dApp interfaces incorporate security measures such as Subresource Integrity, code signing, and source verification to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Frontend applications may be compromised or malicious.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement SRI, code signing, and source authenticity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security scan reports, code signing certificates.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and request validation on APIs and smart contract functions
checklist_items.description: Apply rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and validation checks on critical endpoints to prevent flooding and resource exhaustion attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attackers can flood the system with high-volume requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure API gateways and smart contract gating mechanisms with rate limits and validation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Rate limiting policies, attack simulation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all multisig cancellation transactions are verified against cryptographic signatures from authorized Guardian keys to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust signature verification mechanisms for multisig transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature validation and verification processes.

checklist_items.title: Enforce meticulous verification of transaction parameters in Guardian multisig processes
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig signers to rigorously verify the transaction details (e.g., proposal ID, target account) before signing to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Other signers might not meticulously verify all transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish standard operating procedures for detailed verification prior to signing multisig transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction records with detailed verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance System smart contracts for vulnerability in proposal cancellation
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of smart contracts governing proposal cancellation to prevent vulnerabilities that could be exploited for elevation of privilege or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System allows a transaction signed by the Guardian for cancellation to perform unintended actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform security audits and formal verification on smart contract code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement access control checks and upgradability procedures for Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure Reward Vault contracts have strict access control mechanisms (e.g., onlyOwner modifiers) and upgradability controls with timelocks to prevent unauthorized or malicious modifications, mitigating elevation of privilege risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Reward Vault's access control logic could allow unauthorized actor to manipulate rewards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply rigorous access control and upgrade governance with timelocks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review and deployment records with access control summaries.

checklist_items.title: Mandate strict signature verification for governance proposal and vote transactions
checklist_items.description: Require cryptographic signatures from $BGT token holders for proposal creation and voting to prevent spoofing, ensuring authenticity of governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The signature verification mechanism within the Governance System has a flaw that could be exploited for spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce strong cryptographic signature requirements and verification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature validation logs and code review reports.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Guardian multisig private keys are managed with robust security practices
checklist_items.description: Implement strong key management, hardware security modules, and regular audits for all Guardian multisig keys to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, key management policies, and security review logs.
checklist_items.notes: Regular key rotation and multi-layered security controls are recommended to mitigate key compromise risks.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction parameter verification for multisig cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig members to meticulously verify transaction parameters, especially the proposal ID, before signing, through secure communication channels.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs showing verification steps.
checklist_items.notes: Consider implementing transaction hashing with non-malleable identifiers and off-chain verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures to ensure Guardian key availability and timely cancellation execution
checklist_items.description: Develop backup procedures, geographic diversification, and operational protocols to prevent Guardian unavailability from delaying malicious proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig fails to achieve the required signature threshold (5 of 9) to cancel a genuinely malicious proposal within its timelock period due to unavailability, compromise, or internal disagreement among signers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures for Guardian members, including key backup and recovery plans, clear communication protocols, and designated backup signers if feasible. Ensure geographical and operational diversity among signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedure documentation, backup key distribution logs.
checklist_items.notes: Regular drills and operational reviews are recommended to ensure readiness.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities related to privileged functions and proposal cancellation logic
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits emphasizing access control, reentrancy, and privilege escalation vulnerabilities in Governance System smart contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's legitimate cancellation capability, could inadvertently grant the Guardian (or the transaction itself) unintended powers over the system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results.
checklist_items.notes: Integrate formal verification tools and formalize privilege boundaries in contract code.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance proposals and votes.
checklist_items.description: Ensure all governance proposals and voting transactions are validated through cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system's signature verification mechanism has a flaw or is not enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce rigorous signature verification and audit the governance contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing successful signature verification for proposals and votes.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all transaction parameters, especially in proposal and vote signing, are included in and protected by user signatures (e.g., via EIP-712).
checklist_items.description: Require users to sign all critical transaction parameters, including proposal content and voting options, to prevent tampering during the signing process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: All proposal/vote data are not explicitly included in signed messages, allowing tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive message signing protocols that include all critical parameters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed messages showing inclusion of all relevant parameters.

checklist_items.title: Verify that fallback and recovery procedures are in place for validator private keys.
checklist_items.description: Establish and regularly test secure key management, multi-signature controls, and recovery procedures to prevent malicious or accidental loss of validator keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored insecurely or lack proper controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature Wallets and hardware security modules, along with key rotation policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports and test logs.

checklist_items.title: Audit the smart contracts governing reward distribution for proper access controls and logic correctness.
checklist_items.description: Perform thorough security audits and formal verification of reward vault and incentive distribution contracts to prevent unauthorized manipulation or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vault smart contracts contain vulnerabilities or backdoors.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct audits and implement formal verification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, and test case results.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and secure session management for all frontend interfaces including BeraHub and the NFT bridge.
checklist_items.description: Ensure users verify the authenticity of the interfaces and establish secure sessions to prevent impersonation and hijacking attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be deceived by fraudulent websites/interfaces attempting phishing or impersonation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users, deploy domain verification, and implement browser warnings and multi-factor authentication.

checklist_items.title: Configure all critical transactions to be signed with hardware wallets or secure signing modules, and educate users on verifying transaction details.
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering or malicious signing by ensuring users use hardware wallets and verify all transaction details before approval.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Wallet software can be malicious or compromised to alter transaction data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet usage, provide clear transaction review UI, and conduct user education.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User transaction approval records and security awareness training completion logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all governance proposals and votes.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals and votes are authenticated through strong cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing, as the system relies on signature verification to authenticate user actions.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validators.
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), multisignatures, and secure storage practices to protect validator private keys, mitigating risks associated with key compromise and spoofing validator identities.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices for validators.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of smart contracts.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and network protections.

checklist_items.title: Verify all critical transaction parameters are included in cryptographically signed messages.
checklist_items.description: Ensure the wallet app uses standards like EIP-712 to include all critical parameters in the signed data when claiming airdrops or bridging assets, preventing tampering during transmission.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use EIP-712 or similar standards to include all critical parameters in signatures.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions

checklist_items.description: Ensure that all critical claim parameters are incorporated into the wallet's signing process to prevent tampering or Man-in-the-Middle modifications before the user signs the transaction.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure, standardized signing protocols that include all transaction details.

checklist_items.title: Configure Web Application Firewalls (WAF), rate limits, and anti-DDoS protections on the airdrop claim endpoints.

checklist_items.title: Apply strict input validation and validation for reward configuration data in the reward distributor.
checklist_items.description: Validate all reward allocation configurations received by the distributor to ensure they conform to expected formats, totals, and constraints, avoiding malformed or malicious data that could disrupt reward logic or cause unintended effects.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure verification procedures for cancellation transaction parameters within the multisig process
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing, using secure communication channels and non-malleable transaction identifiers.

checklist_items.description: Implement user awareness campaigns, official URL bookmarks, and wallet warnings to mitigate phishing risks for airdrop claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, phishing awareness campaign records.

checklist_items.title: Implement end-to-end transaction signing with included critical parameters for airdrop claims
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all critical claim parameters, such as recipient address and nonce, are included in the signed message (e.g., EIP-712), and verified on-chain to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure. The frontend of the Airdrop Claim Application itself is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed messages and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure on-chain eligibility check endpoints with authentication, rate limiting, and data minimization
checklist_items.description: Implement strong access controls, authentication measures, and limit information leakage in eligibility check APIs to prevent data disclosure and enumeration attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The eligibility check endpoint of the Airdrop Claim Application allows querying by arbitrary wallet addresses or social identifiers without strong authentication or rate limiting.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication for eligibility checks. Only return eligibility status (and amount if applicable) for the authenticated user. Apply rate limiting and CAPTCHAs to eligibility check endpoints to prevent mass querying. Avoid using easily guessable or enumerable identifiers in URLs or API calls related to allocation data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: API access logs, rate-limiting configuration documentation.

checklist_items.title: Apply robust rate limiting and anti-spam measures to prevent DoS attacks on the airdrop claim process
checklist_items.description: Implement API rate limits, CAPTCHA challenges, and network protections on the airdrop claim endpoints to mitigate flooding and resource exhaustion attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks. Employ Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS protection services for the frontend. Optimize smart contract gas usage to make abuse costly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Traffic logs showing rate limiting measures and incident reports.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control and input validation in smart contracts to prevent privilege escalation in airdrop related functions
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough security audits on smart contracts controlling incentive rates and other critical parameters, applying least privilege principles and formal verification techniques.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) has admin functions for incentive management protected by an owner/manager role. The Protocol Admin (Actor 5) uses an EOA (Token Manager wallet) whose private key can be stolen.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong private key management for the Token Manager EOA. Use hardware wallets. Consider multisig for critical incentive management functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, key management procedures documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature controls and timelocks for critical incentive configuration changes
checklist_items.description: Require multiple approvals and enforced delays before applying significant reward or incentive parameter updates to prevent malicious or unauthorized tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Actor 5 (Token Manager EOA) acts maliciously or acts maliciously if compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement timelocks for significant rate changes. Use multisig for admin controls. Regular audits of admin activity and configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs, governance voting records, timelock execution reports.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and audit reward distribution operations to detect anomalies and unauthorized changes
checklist_items.description: Implement continuous monitoring and regular audits of reward-related transactions and configuration changes to swiftly identify and respond to tampering or privilege escalation attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Actor 5 (Token Manager EOA) makes an incentive deposit or rate change and later denies having authorized the transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain clear and secure operational procedures for the Token Manager EOA. Use multisig to distribute responsibility. Publicly communicate significant changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, transaction history reports, anomaly detection alerts.

checklist_items.title: Upgrade Reward Vault smart contracts with security best practices and formal verification
checklist_items.description: Develop and deploy Reward Vault contracts following proven secure design principles, conduct formal verification if possible, and perform thorough audits before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) smart contract contains a bug in its incentive management functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough security audits of Reward Vault smart contracts. Implement gas limits and checks against reentrancy or unbounded loops. Upgradability mechanism for bug fixes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement access control mechanisms with role-based permissions in Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Design Reward Vaults with strict access controls, utilizing established libraries (e.g., OpenZeppelin) to prevent unauthorized administration of incentives and funds.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Reward Vault's access control logic could allow an unauthorized actor to execute administrative functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits focusing on access control. Use well-tested libraries for ownership/role management (e.g., OpenZeppelin). Minimize the attack surface of admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, access control audit records.

checklist_items.title: Verify that bridge smart contracts include anti-fraud and anti-spoofing measures
checklist_items.description: Audit bridge contracts to include verification steps, whitelists, and signature checks to ensure only legitimate assets and addresses are processed during cross-chain asset transfers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A malicious or compromised frontend/wallet alters the parameters of the 'Asset Bridging Transaction' before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct rigorous security audits of the Berachain Bridge contracts. Implement strict signature verification, whitelisting, and digest validation. Educate users on verifying bridge contract addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, transaction validation logs, user report records.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction validation and signature verification in bridge smart contracts to prevent tampering
checklist_items.description: Design bridge contracts to verify the authenticity and integrity of bridging transactions, ensuring parameters have not been altered after signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend is compromised, or the user does not verify the recipient address and other critical details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure BeraHub frontend integrity, encourage users to verify all transaction details (especially recipient addresses) in their trusted wallet interface.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction signature verification logs, user verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Use secure and verified interfaces for cross-chain bridge interactions to prevent spoofing
checklist_items.description: Guide users to interact only through official, verified bridge interfaces and avoid untrusted sources, alongside user education to raise awareness of phishing risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User 7 might click on a phishing link leading to a fake bridge interface.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official bookmarks, wallet warnings for malicious sites, user education on bridge security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training records, phishing awareness campaign documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify ownership and control of Guardian multisig private keys with hardware security modules or multisig hardware wallets.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Guardian multisig employs hardware security modules (HSMs) or multisig hardware wallets to protect private keys from theft or compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of multisig key management and hardware wallet deployment.

checklist_items.title: Require all Guardian multisig signers to meticulously verify transaction parameters, especially cancellation targets, before signing.
checklist_items.description: Establish formal processes and checklist procedures for multisig signers to verify transaction details, including target proposals, to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Records of signer verification logs or signed transaction checksums.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities related to proposal cancellation functions, focusing on access control and reentrancy vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of smart contracts governing proposal cancellations to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could lead to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's legitimate cancellation capability, could inadvertently grant the Guardian (or the transaction itself) unintended powers over the system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, incorrect access control, and privilege escalation vulnerabilities. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, or vulnerability scan reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict input validation on all Governance System configuration transactions, including reward allocations, with formal verification and rejection of malformed data.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that reward configuration inputs are validated against predefined schemas and parameters to prevent tampering or malformed data from causing unintended behaviors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system can gracefully handle or reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor contract and any L1 logic that processes this data. Reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validation logs, static analysis reports, or schema validation test results.

checklist_items.title: Enforce cryptographic signatures on all governance proposals and votes, and educate users on verifying signatures and transaction authenticity.
checklist_items.description: Require that all governance actions are signed with cryptographic signatures validated on-chain, and provide user guidance to verify signer identities before approving actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can exploit a vulnerability in the user's wallet or the signing process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandatory strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions, secure key management by users, regular audits of Governance System contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signature verification logs, user education documentation, or audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Use official, verified interfaces with domain validation and user warnings for all cross-chain or bridge interactions.
checklist_items.description: Educate users to only use official websites and verify contract addresses before initiating bridging transactions, supported by wallet warnings and website validation measures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User 7 might click on a phishing link leading to a fake bridge interface.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official and verified interfaces for bridging, verify contract addresses through multiple trusted sources, implement clear warnings and educational prompts in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs, browser security audit reports, or educational material references.

checklist_items.title: Require end-to-end encryption and integrity checks for all off-chain transaction data, especially parameters for NFT bridging, to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.description: Implement mechanisms such as EIP-712 signatures or on-chain commitments to ensure that transaction data, including recipient addresses, are signed and verified, preventing offline tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User 7's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS and implement integrity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message payloads, implementation of EIP-712 signatures, or code review reports.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract and Frontend Security Team

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and request throttling on all critical API endpoints for airdrop eligibility checks and claims.
checklist_items.description: Deploy web application firewalls and limit the request rate from individual users/IPs to prevent abuse and flooding attacks that could lead to denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's backend lacks sufficient protection against request flooding.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and DDoS protections for the frontend and backend.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Server logs showing rate limiting enforcement, CDN configuration, or firewall rules.

checklist_items.title: Use secure session management, multi-factor authentication, and session timeout policies for all Airdrop Claim Application user interactions.
checklist_items.description: Implement session management best practices, including token expiration, secure cookies, and 2FA where applicable, to prevent session hijacking and impersonation attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The eligibility check endpoint allows querying by arbitrary addresses without strong authentication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication, session management, rate limiting, and MFA where possible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Session logs, MFA enrollment records, or security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisig security for Guardian multisig wallets
checklist_items.description: Use multisig wallets with a sufficient number of secure private keys and frequent audits to prevent compromise of Guardian multisig, which can lead to spoofing or tampering threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of multisig private keys management protocols and recent security audits.

checklist_items.title: Require comprehensive transaction verification procedures among Guardian multisig signers
checklist_items.description: Establish process controls for all Guardian multisig signers to meticulously verify all transaction parameters before signing, including transaction details and targets, to prevent tampering or data alteration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Records of transaction verification logs and signatures.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and backups for Guardian multisig members
checklist_items.description: Develop operational protocols, including key management, insider threat mitigation, and backup signers, to maintain availability and prevent unintentional denial of cancellation capacity in high-risk scenarios.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig fails to achieve the required signature threshold due to unavailability, compromise, or internal disagreement.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures for Guardian members, including key backup and recovery plans, clear communication protocols, and designated backup signers if feasible. Ensure geographical and operational diversity among signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of operational procedures, backup key management plans, and incident response protocols.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance System smart contracts thoroughly
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive security audits and formal verification of all governance smart contracts related to proposal creation, voting, and cancellation functions to identify and fix vulnerabilities exploitable for privilege escalation or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect access control) exists in the Governance System's smart contracts related to proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and security testing documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong key management and multi-factor authentication for Validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Use hardware wallets, multisig, or secure key management practices for Validator private keys to prevent compromise and spoofing of block proposal or validator actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices for validators. Monitor for anomalous staking activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documents, key access logs, hardware wallet usage audits.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit validator node infrastructure and implement DoS protections
checklist_items.description: Employ network security practices, monitor for unusual traffic, and implement DDoS mitigation measures on validator nodes to prevent DoS attacks that can prevent participation in consensus or block proposing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators are responsible for maintaining the uptime and security of their own node infrastructure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Validators should implement robust operational security, including DoS protection for their nodes. The network should have mechanisms to penalize prolonged validator downtime (slashing).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of network security measures, DDoS mitigation logs, uptime monitoring reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict input validation and validation of state-changing transactions in smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that reward configuration transactions and reward calculation logic include validation checks for data correctness, proper signatures, and adherence to expected parameters to prevent tampering or malicious modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: If L1 (Component 101) and Distributor logic do not validate reward configuration data properly, malformed data could disrupt PoL reward logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor contract (116) and any L1 (101) logic that processes this data. Reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validation logs, test cases demonstrating input validation, formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure transaction signing and user verification practices
checklist_items.description: Encourage use of hardware wallets, multi-factor authentication, and clear transaction review interfaces to prevent signing malicious or tampered transactions, especially for critical operations like redemption and delegation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The User Wallet Software (107) is malicious or has been compromised and tampers with transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use reputable and audited wallet software, hardware wallets that provide a trusted display for transaction verification, careful transaction review.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, wallet usage guidelines, reports of invalid transaction signatures.

checklist_items.title: Verify all critical transaction parameters are included in cryptographically signed messages
checklist_items.description: Design the wallet and signing protocols so that all sensitive and critical parameters (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the signed message to prevent tampering during transit or signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: All critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of signing protocols, signature generation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education and warnings about token approvals
checklist_items.description: Notify users about the implications of granting unlimited token approval, avoid 'approve all' patterns, and encourage revoking unnecessary permissions regularly.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User (2) may not understand the full implications of permissions they grant.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education on token approvals, wallets providing clear warnings and approval management tools (e.g., revoke.cash integration), dApps requesting minimal necessary approvals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User education materials, UI/UX review reports, approval activity logs.

checklist_items.title: Educate users to verify official URLs and enforce domain and resource integrity
checklist_items.description: Instruct users to access official dApps through trusted URLs, employ HTTPS with valid certificates, and avoid clicking links from untrusted sources to prevent phishing and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback, phishing awareness training completion reports.

checklist_items.title: Apply rate limiting and anti-DoS protections on application's API and smart contract functions
checklist_items.description: Implement throttling, CAPTCHAs, or other request management techniques to prevent request flooding and resource exhaustion attacks against the Airdrop Claim Application and gateway endpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: API logs showing rate limiting in effect, traffic analysis reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature verification for Guardian multisig transactions to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that multisig signatures are properly validated and stored securely to prevent unauthorized actions or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on cryptographic identities of Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed multisig transaction records and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Require meticulous verification of transaction parameters within Guardian multisig before signing.
checklist_items.description: Establish process controls that mandate each Guardian signer to verify all transaction details, especially target proposals, prior to co-signature.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig is used to authorize proposal cancellations, which can be tampered with if signers do not verify parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction data with verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Ensure timely submission and execution of Guardian multisig cancellation transactions within the timelock period.
checklist_items.description: Implement operational procedures and backup mechanisms to guarantee that enough Guardian signers are available to reach threshold and cancel malicious proposals promptly.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig needs at least 5 of 9 signatures within the proposal's timelock period to prevent malicious execution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures, key backups, and operational diversity among signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational logs demonstrating timely transaction submissions.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities that could allow privilege escalation during guardian-initiated cancellations.
checklist_items.description: Perform code reviews, security audits, and formal verification on the governance smart contracts, focusing on access control and vulnerability to exploitations that could elevate privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in governance contracts may allow the Guardian's cancellation actions to have unintended effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits, focus on access control mechanisms, apply least privilege principles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, vulnerability scans, formal verification documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement thorough validation of proposal cancellation parameters before execution.
checklist_items.description: Design the smart contract logic such that all parameters of cancellation transactions are validated against expected formats and ranges to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Tampering with transaction data can lead to incorrect proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce input validation checks on all critical parameters within the smart contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code, validation tests, transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Configure and deploy governance smart contracts with transparent, well-tested upgrade mechanisms and timelocks.
checklist_items.description: Apply governance controls, such as timelocks and multi-party approval, to critical functions to prevent unauthorized or rapid privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities or misconfigurations in the governance setup could lead to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use timelocks, governance voting, and multi-signature controls for critical upgrades.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Deployment logs, governance decision records, upgrade proposals.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance proposal and vote submissions.
checklist_items.description: Implement rigorous signature checks in the governance contract logic to prevent spoofed proposals or votes from unauthorized actors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Signature verification mechanisms may be flawed or bypassable, enabling spoofing of governance actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure cryptographic libraries, adhere to best practices in signature verification, and perform regular audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, cryptographic verification proofs.

checklist_items.title: Secure wallet private key management, including hardware wallets and multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users manage their private keys securely to prevent compromise leading to elevation of privilege or theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys or seed phrases stored on compromised user devices or malicious software can lead to asset theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet use, educate users, and incorporate key management best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User attestations, security protocol documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement user interface protections to prevent transaction tampering, such as WYSIWYS (What You See Is What You Sign) and verified transaction details.
checklist_items.description: Use hardware wallets and wallet UI features to ensure that users verify all transaction details, especially in sensitive actions like redemption or token approval.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious or compromised wallet software can display incorrect transaction information, leading to unintended asset transfer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use reputable wallets with clear transaction review, educate users on verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction screenshots, wallet audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Educate users on verifying contract addresses and URLs to prevent phishing and spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.description: Distribute educational materials warning users to confirm official URLs and contract addresses before interacting with dApps or bridges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by phishing sites or malicious interfaces impersonating legitimate dApps.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Official communication channels, community alerts, user awareness campaigns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback, phishing awareness reports.

checklist_items.title: Use official, verified sources for contract addresses, domain names, and interface links.
checklist_items.description: Implement measures such as DNSSEC, HTTPS, and domain monitoring to ensure users only interact with legitimate dApp interfaces and bridge URLs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users rely on unverified sources, enabling attackers to impersonate dApps.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt secure domain registration practices, verify contract deployment addresses, and employ security pins or extended validation certificates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Domain registration records, HTTPS certificate logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and request validation for critical endpoints and smart contracts to prevent flooding and request spamming.
checklist_items.description: Design the dApp backend and smart contracts to limit requests per user/IP and validate input data rigorously for functions like staking, bridging, or governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts and dApp backend may be vulnerable to DoS attacks without proper safeguards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply rate limits, input validation, and consider off-chain moderation or CAPTCHA mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Request logs, rate limiting configurations, attack simulation reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control and permission checks in smart contracts to prevent unauthorized administrative operations or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.description: Use role-based access control (RBAC) patterns and conduct formal verification to ensure only authorized accounts can perform privileged actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract access control mechanisms may be flawed or bypassed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt proven access control libraries (e.g., OpenZeppelin Ownable/AccessControl), perform security audits, and enforce multi-party approvals for critical functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Design and enforce robust formal verification and security auditing processes for all smart contracts, especially those handling token distribution and governance.
checklist_items.description: Use formal methods and third-party audits to identify potential exploits in contracts which can be used for privilege escalation, tampering, or privilege misuse.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities that attackers could exploit for privilege escalation or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use formal verification tools and third-party audits before deployment and periodically thereafter.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification logs, audit certification documents.

checklist_items.title: Encourage and enable the use of hardware wallets and multi-factor authentication for wallet private key management.
checklist_items.description: Adopt hardware wallets and MFA for transaction signing and private key storage to reduce the risk of private key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys stored insecurely or on compromised devices can lead to asset theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet adoption, conduct user security training, and reinforce best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet setup documentation, user attestation.

checklist_items.title: Implement WYSIWYS (What You See Is What You Sign) transaction review features in wallets for sensitive actions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure wallet UI displays all transaction details comprehensively, and users verify critical data such as recipient addresses before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious or compromised wallets may alter transaction details or misrepresent information to the user.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Utilize reputable wallets with transparent transaction review features, educate users on verifying transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction confirmation screenshots, wallet audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and augment smart contract code with formal verification to prevent privilege escalation through token approval manipulation.
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive audits on token approval mechanisms to prevent unwarranted permissions leading to privilege escalation or asset theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unwanted privilege escalation occurs if token approval processes are flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use well-established approval patterns, provide approval revocation tools, and audit code regularly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, approval logs, security assessments.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Guardian multisig keys are secured with hardware security modules (HSMs) and regular key rotations.
checklist_items.description: Implement robust key management practices for Guardian multisig to prevent compromise, as assumptions include control over multisig keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig relies on cryptographic key integrity; there is a risk if keys are compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules for key storage, enforce regular key rotations, and conduct periodic key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing key storage security and key rotation records.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure that guardians have access to hardware wallets and enforce multi-factor authentication.

checklist_items.title: Verify all Guardian multisig transaction parameters are meticulously checked and validated before signing.
checklist_items.description: Assumptions include that signers might not verify transaction details; process control mitigations involve thorough verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious signers might modify transaction data before approval.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement detailed review protocols requiring signers to verify all transaction fields prior to signing, possibly with dedicated transaction review tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs with reviewer comments or approval sheets.
checklist_items.notes: Incorporate multi-party review procedures for transaction parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and alerting on transaction signatures to detect anomalies in Guardian multisig signing activity.
checklist_items.description: Assumes risk of insufficient operational monitoring; process controls include signature activity monitoring.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unusual signing patterns may indicate a security breach or internal misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy real-time analytics on multisig transaction logs with automated alerting for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards and alert logs.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure logging of all multisig signature transactions and set up alerts for suspicious activity.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive smart contract audits for BEX, Reward Vaults, and Governance modules to identify and fix vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.description: Mitigates risk of exploits and tampering in smart contracts; assumptions include vulnerabilities in code if not audited.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain bugs or vulnerabilities if not properly audited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors for thorough review and formal verification of smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and certification documents.
checklist_items.notes: Prioritize third-party audits and avoid deploying unverified smart contract code.

checklist_items.title: Develop and enforce operational protocols for Guardian multisig operations, including regular backups, key rotation, and access controls.
checklist_items.description: Reduces actor and trust boundary risks associated with operational failures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Operational failures (e.g., key loss or unavailability) could prevent Guardian actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish documented operational procedures, conduct regular drills, and implement backup signing keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedure documentation, backup key rotation logs.
checklist_items.notes: Include procedures for key recovery and incident response.

checklist_items.title: Set up continuous smart contract monitoring for BEX, Reward Vaults, Governance modules to detect anomalies and possible tampering.
checklist_items.description: Assumes risk of undetected tampering; continuous monitoring can detect suspicious activities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities that could be exploited without detection.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement monitoring tools that analyze transaction patterns and contract state changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system logs and alerts.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure alerts trigger on suspicious activity patterns.

checklist_items.title: Use cryptographically secure randomness sources for administrator and validator operations to prevent predictability and manipulation.
checklist_items.description: Assumption addresses threat of tampering and elevation of privilege through predictable randomness; mitigate by using CSPRNGs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Operator or validator randomness should be generated securely to prevent manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate hardware entropy sources or using well-tested CSPRNG libraries verified for cryptographic security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Source code review, entropy source documentation.
checklist_items.notes: Use hardware-based entropy sources where possible.

checklist_items.title: Implement domain-based authentication and communication security measures for off-chain components to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.description: Assumptions include that off-chain components may be vulnerable to impersonation; mitigate by enforcing domain validation, TLS, and code signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain communication channels may be vulnerable to spoofing or tampering without domain validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce TLS with verification of server certificates, implement code signing, and domain whitelisting for off-chain integrations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: TLS configuration audits, code signing certificates, domain validation logs.
checklist_items.notes: Apply TLS and verify server certificates for all off-chain communication.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls for off-chain component APIs and interfaces, including API keys, IP whitelisting, and role-based permissions.
checklist_items.description: Assumption is that weak access controls can lead to spoofing or unauthorized manipulation; address by implementing robust access policies.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain components may not be properly secured, enabling impersonation or unauthorized access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control with least privilege, IP whitelisting, and API key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policy documents, audit logs showing enforcement.
checklist_items.notes: Use API keys and IP whitelisting to restrict access to off-chain APIs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig cancellation transactions are validated against cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust signature verification mechanisms within the Governance System smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature verification steps during proposal cancellation.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and contingency plans for Guardian multisig key management
checklist_items.description: Create and follow strict operational procedures, including regular audits, key backups, and diversification, to prevent unavailability of Guardian multisig keys that could hinder malicious proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Fewer than 5 Guardian multisig members may be unavailable or compromised, affecting cancellation ability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement operational best practices for multisig key management, including diversification and regular audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of operational procedures and recent multisig audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive smart contract security audits for the Governance System and Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Perform in-depth audits, including formal verification if possible, on the Governance System and Reward Vault smart contracts to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could be exploited for elevation of privilege or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in Governance System smart contracts may allow unintended effects or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security firms for audits and formal verification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports detailing identified vulnerabilities and resolution steps.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong access control mechanisms and least privilege principle in Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized entities can invoke administrative functions in Reward Vaults, using multisignature wallets, role-based access control, and time delays for sensitive operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault smart contracts might have flawed access control allowing unauthorized function execution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use established access control patterns (e.g., OpenZeppelin's Ownable/Role-based) with multisig governance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and access control audit results.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validator keys and establish multi-factor authentication
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), multisignature schemes, and hardware wallets for validator private keys to prevent compromise and unauthorized signing that could lead to impersonation or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored in a manner susceptible to compromise, enabling spoofing of block proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure key storage and management, including hardware wallets and multi-party thresholds.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies and hardware wallet usage records.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction origin verification and session security for governance and delegation portals
checklist_items.description: Enforce strict frontend security, including HTTPS, code signing, and anti-phishing measures, plus verify transaction signatures from legitimate wallets to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance and delegation interfaces could be compromised, enabling spoofed transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use signed messages from wallets, verify signatures on-chain, and educate users to verify transaction details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of signature verification and user confirmation screens.

checklist_items.title: Validate all relevant transaction parameters within smart contracts before execution
checklist_items.description: Require on-chain verification that all critical parameters (e.g., recipient address, amount, proposal ID) are included in signed messages and verified within the smart contract to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may accept transactions with tampered parameters if validation is insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict parameter validation and signature checks within smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code review and test case results confirming validation.

checklist_items.title: Use HTTPS, code signing, and user education to prevent phishing of wallet interfaces and bridge sites
checklist_items.description: Ensure all user-facing applications, sites, and communications employ HTTPS with certificate validation, implement code signing, and educate users to verify URLs and contract addresses to prevent spoofing and social engineering.—
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be deceived by malicious or spoofed frontends of bridge and dApp sites.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official URLs, verify certificates, and educate users on recognizing legitimate sites.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Web security configuration, user training materials, and phishing awareness reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict on-chain validation of reward allocation configurations
checklist_items.description: Validate all parameters in reward configuration transactions, including vault addresses and weight totals, before applying changes to prevent malformed or malicious data from disrupting PoL rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward allocation configurations could be maliciously crafted and accepted without validation, causing PoL issues.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce input validation, checks for valid addresses, and safeguard against overflow or incorrect sums.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract validation code and test cases demonstrating rejection of invalid configurations.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Guardian multisig keys are managed with hardware security modules or best practices
checklist_items.description: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members to prevent unauthorized control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of multisig key access and management procedures.

checklist_items.title: Establish transaction parameter verification and secure communication channels for Guardian multisig actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing, and use secure channels for communication.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of transaction parameter verification processes and secure communication records.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance System smart contracts for security vulnerabilities, focusing on proposal cancellation functions
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, especially proposal cancellation logic, and apply formal verification where feasible.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect access control) exists in the Governance System's smart contracts related to proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations in Distributor and L1 modules
checklist_items.description: Ensure all reward allocation configuration data is validated for correctness and completeness before being processed or applied.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Distributor component (116) and/or L1 (101) logic consuming this configuration performs validation on the input (e.g., valid vault addresses, sum of weights).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor contract (116) and any L1 (101) logic that processes this data. Reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test cases and audit logs showing validation logic enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit reward calculation and distribution mechanisms for fairness and correctness
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough audits and formal verification (if feasible) of reward logic in the L1 (Component 101) and related modules to prevent exploits and ensure proper reward attribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The L1 (Component 101) correctly calculates and attributes all forms of validator rewards (fees, $BGT, incentives via integrated components).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough auditing and formal verification (if feasible) of all smart contracts and L1 modules involved in reward calculation and distribution. Implement strong access controls and invariant checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification documentation, and on-chain reward distribution consistency checks.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust key management and regular audits for Guardian multisig wallets.
checklist_items.description: Ensure multisig keys are stored securely and regularly audited to prevent compromise, aligning with mitigation strategy for Spoofing threats targeting multisig.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of multisig key management and recent security audit reports.
checklist_items.owner: Security Engineering Team and Key Management Team

checklist_items.title: Ensure transaction parameters for proposal cancellations are meticulously verified by all Guardian multisig signers.
checklist_items.description: Encourage signers to verify all details of cancellation transactions before signing, preventing tampering as per mitigation strategy for Tampering threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all transaction parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction data with recorded verification logs from signers.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts thoroughly for vulnerabilities, especially in proposal cancellation logic.
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit and consider formal verification of smart contracts related to governance proposals, aligning with mitigation strategy for Actor Privilege Escalation threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's legitimate cancellation capability, could inadvertently grant the Guardian (or the transaction itself) unintended powers over the system.
