checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, access control, and vulnerability patterns. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports, formal verification results, and code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions involving $BGT.
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic signature requirements to authenticate governance proposals and votes, consistent with mitigation for Spoofing and Elevation of Privilege threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The $BGT Holder's identity can be spoofed if signature verification is flawed or keys are compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographically secure signatures for all governance-related transactions. Use wallet integration that enforces signature verification. Regularly audit signature validation logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed transactions with verification logs, cryptography proofs.

checklist_items.title: Secure wallet private key management with hardware wallets, multisig, and strong operational procedures.
checklist_items.description: Adopt best practices for private key security to prevent compromise, aligning with mitigation for Tampering and Spoofing threats targeting private keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User wallets are responsible for securely managing private keys and seed phrases. Weaknesses here can lead to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, enforce multi-factor authentication, conduct user education, and implement strict operational procedures for key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet deployment records, user training records.

checklist_items.title: Validate all on-chain reward configuration data before deployment, ensuring correctness and completeness.
checklist_items.description: Implement strict validation for reward allocation parameters before submission to prevent malformed data exploits, aligning with mitigation for Tampering threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The reward configuration data can be maliciously crafted or malformed and accepted if not validated.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement input validation, schema checks, and constraints in the reward configuration process within the Distributor contract and involved L1 modules.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validation logs, sample configuration inputs, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature controls for Guardian multisig wallet to prevent spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Guardian multisig wallet (component 109) is managed with robust multisignature protocols (e.g., >=5 of 9 signers) to mitigate the risk of actors compromising fewer private keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of multisig wallet security protocols and signatures from a threshold number of guardians.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict transaction parameter verification in multisig processes for proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig signers to meticulously verify all transaction details (e.g., target proposal ID) before signing to prevent tampering with cancellation parameters.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious Guardian signer could modify transaction data before signatures are collected.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members verify all parameters before signing. Use secure communication channels. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs showing verification steps and signatures from all signers.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures for Guardian multisig availability, including key management and backup plans.
checklist_items.description: Develop and regularly update procedures to ensure Guardian multisig can achieve the required threshold (>=5 of 9) signatures within the timelock period, including key backups and diverse geographic distribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Failure of Guardian multisig to reach consensus due to unavailability or internal disputes can prevent malicious proposals from being canceled.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish operational protocols, backup signing procedures, and diversify Guardian operational team locations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational plan documentation, participation logs, and backup key deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contract code focusing on vulnerability to vulnerabilities that could enable privilege escalation or unintended influence.
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of the Governance System smart contracts (component 109), especially those related to proposal cancellation, to identify and remediate potential privilege escalation flaws.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System could allow the Guardian's cancellation action to have unintended broader effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports with vulnerability findings and remediation steps.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, encrypted communication channels for Guardian multisig transaction coordination.
checklist_items.description: Use secure, encrypted communication methods (e.g., TLS, VPNs) for the coordination and signing of multisig transactions to mitigate risks of intercepting or tampering with transaction data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Tampering with transaction data before it is signed can lead to malicious cancellation or parameter alteration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian communications are encrypted and authenticated.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network security logs demonstrating encryption protocols in use during transaction coordination.

checklist_items.title: Configure the blockchain network with mechanisms such as frequent checkpoints, dark-fork detection, or community monitoring to detect and respond to privilege escalation attempts.
checklist_items.description: Implement network and governance-level monitoring to detect abnormal or malicious activities, such as unusual proposal creation or voting patterns that could indicate privilege escalation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Governance System's smart contracts could allow a $BGT Holder to create unauthorized proposals or votes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement monitoring, alerting, and incident response protocols for governance activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, monitoring dashboards, alerts triggered by suspicious activity.

checklist_items.title: Educate users on verifying official URLs, contract addresses, and transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.description: Develop user awareness campaigns and wallet prompts to help users identify legitimate interfaces of dApps like BEX, BeraHub, Honey Swap, and avoid falling for phishing or spoofing websites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking official dApps.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Official communications, verified links, user training, and browser extension warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback, phishing simulation reports, browser security block logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig cancellation transactions are verified through cryptographic signature validation to prevent spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce signature verification in multisig contract or process
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature validation enforcement on multisig transactions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all Guardian multisig transaction parameters are meticulously verified before signing
checklist_items.description: Establish procedures and controls to verify transaction details, such as cancellation target proposal ID, before sign-off by each multisig signer.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: All Guardian multisig members verify all transaction parameters meticulously before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure communication channels and non-malleable transaction formats.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs showing parameter verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Audit and formally verify the security of Governance System smart contracts, focusing on proposal cancellation mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of the Governance System contracts to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could allow elevation of privilege or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System has potential smart contract vulnerabilities allowing privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Formal verification and third-party audits of smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates showing no critical vulnerabilities.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls and minimize privileges in Reward Vault smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Review and reinforce access control mechanisms within Reward Vault contracts to prevent unauthorized modifications or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts have potential flaws in their access control logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use established access control libraries, and apply the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing role assignments and access control checks.

checklist_items.title: Conduct security audits focusing on the smart contract logic of reward and incentive mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Ensure all reward management, incentive rate adjustments, and distribution functions are secure, correct, and free from logic errors through thorough audits and formal verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault and related contracts have potential vulnerabilities or logic flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular audits, formal verification, and bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification results, and bug bounty findings.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure key management and multi-factor authentication for validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Implement hardware security modules, multisignature schemes, and operational procedures to protect validator keys against compromise and unauthorized use.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored insecurely or are vulnerable to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets and multisig key schemes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet configurations, and operational procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust private key management practices for Validator nodes
checklist_items.description: Adopt best practices like hardware wallets, secure storage, regular key rotation, and multi-party custody to prevent key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are at risk of theft or compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Utilize hardware security modules, multisig, and strict operational protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational policies, hardware wallet deployment documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enable multi-party signing for critical validator operations
checklist_items.description: Configure multisignature schemes for validator activities such as proposal signing, slashing, or updates to prevent single-point key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator operations depend on single private keys vulnerable to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig signing workflows with multiple secure key holders.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig setup configurations, operational procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement continuous monitoring and anomaly detection for validator activities
checklist_items.description: Set up monitoring systems to detect unusual signature patterns, unauthorized proposals, or key usage anomalies for rapid response.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys or operations may be targeted for attack or misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy monitoring and alerting infrastructure for validator activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard logs, alert configurations.

checklist_items.title: Verify that social login authentication mechanisms are secure and resilient against account compromise
checklist_items.description: Assess and improve social login integrations to prevent account takeovers that could lead to impersonation or unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The airdrop claim system relies on social login with weak security measures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong two-factor authentication and secure account linking procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Authentication system configuration documentation, security assessment reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals and votes are authenticated through cryptographic signatures from the voter's private key to prevent spoofing and integrity breaches.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System's signature verification mechanism has a flaw that could allow spoofed proposals or votes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strengthen cryptographic signature validation protocols and conduct regular cryptographic audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed proposal or vote transactions demonstrating signature validation.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validator nodes and other critical actors
checklist_items.description: Establish robust private key management practices, including hardware security modules (HSMs), multisignature wallets, and regular key rotation for validator and admin keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules, multi-signature schemes, and key rotation policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs, hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Validate all smart contract inputs and enforce strict input validation
checklist_items.description: Implement comprehensive input validation on all smart contract functions, especially reward configuration and incentive management functions, to prevent malformed or malicious data from causing unintended behavior.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The reward configuration system might be vulnerable to malformed data if not validated properly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add strict input validation, input schema enforcement, and rejection of invalid data within the smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test cases demonstrating rejection of malformed reward configuration data.

checklist_items.title: Apply rate limiting and anti-DoS measures on all critical front-end and API endpoints
checklist_items.description: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA challenges, and request validation on all user-facing components and APIs related to staking, delegation, and governance functions to prevent request flooding.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend and associated smart contracts are vulnerable to request flooding if not protected.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy web application firewalls, rate limiting, and CAPTCHA mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Traffic logs showing rate limiting enforcement and CAPTCHA challenge success rates.

checklist_items.title: Educate users on verifying official URLs and contract addresses before interacting
checklist_items.description: Provide clear guidance and warnings to users to verify the authenticity of the websites, dApp interfaces, and smart contract addresses they interact with, to prevent phishing and spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users might be deceived by phishing sites or fake interfaces due to lack of awareness.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Distribute user education materials, warnings in wallets, and official communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Distribution records of user education campaigns and evidence of user verification practices.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain nonce or sequence number checks for transaction replay prevention
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all critical transactions, especially redemption and configuration updates, include nonces or sequence numbers checked on-chain to prevent replay attacks and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The redemption process might be vulnerable to replay attacks if nonces are not enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain nonce or sequence number validation within redemption and critical transaction functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample transaction sequence logs demonstrating nonce usage and validation.

checklist_items.title: Establish monitoring and alerting for abnormal governance activity patterns
checklist_items.description: Implement monitoring on governance proposal and voting activity to detect anomalous patterns indicating potential spoofing, tampering, or automated attack scripts, with alerts for manual review.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unusual patterns in governance activity could indicate attempted tampering or spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy governance activity monitoring tools and anomaly detection systems.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing detection of anomalous proposal/vote activities.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for critical operations and admin functions
checklist_items.description: Enforce MFA for all operations involving admin privileges, including reward configuration, key management, and sensitive governance actions, to reduce risks from compromised private keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The security gap includes risks of key compromise affecting critical system controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement MFA using hardware tokens or hardware security modules for administrative access.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Authentication logs showing MFA enforcement and successful authentications.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and verify smart contract code and deployment process
checklist_items.description: Perform periodic security audits, code reviews, and formal verification of smart contracts responsible for governance, incentive management, and bridging to detect vulnerabilities and prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may contain vulnerabilities that can be exploited for tampering or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct regular audits and formal verification of critical smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and remediation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig transactions are verified using cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement signature validation and multisig recovery procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature verification processes and multisig transaction histories.

checklist_items.title: Secure key management and regular audits for multisig Guardian wallets
checklist_items.description: Establish robust key management practices and conduct periodic audits of multisig wallet contracts to mitigate the risk of compromise or internal threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig relies on the cryptographic security of private keys.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor authentication, hardware security modules, and regular vulnerability assessments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, key management procedures, and recent multisig wallet audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Design proposal parameters to be non-malleable and include all verification data
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all transaction parameters related to proposal cancellation are non-malleable and included within cryptographic signatures to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Other signers might not meticulously verify all transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use non-malleable parameter encoding schemes and enforce comprehensive verification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction data and testing reports demonstrating parameter integrity.

checklist_items.title: Implement process controls requiring meticulous verification of all mitigation transactions
checklist_items.description: Establish operational procedures requiring Guardian multisig members to verify all transaction parameters thoroughly before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Other signers might not verify all transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Create detailed transaction review checklists and peer verification protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Training records, review logs, and incident reports demonstrating adherence.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and diverse backup plans for Guardian key management
checklist_items.description: Develop and implement operational procedures and diversity strategies to ensure Guardian multisig can perform cancellations during operational failures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Operational failures can prevent Guardian multisig actions due to unavailability of signers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Create backup key procedures, geographic dispersion, and operational redundancy plans.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational manuals, backup key audit logs, and training documentation.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive smart contract security audits focusing on access control and privilege escalation
checklist_items.description: Perform thorough security audits of Reward Vaults and incentive management contracts to detect vulnerabilities that may lead to elevation of privilege or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain vulnerabilities or malicious logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors, implement formal verification, and enforce least privilege access models.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, and code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong access control mechanisms with timelocks and multisig safeguards for administrative functions
checklist_items.description: Protect admin functions of Reward Vaults with multisig approvals and timelocks to prevent unauthorized privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults' admin functions are protected by access control, but vulnerabilities exist.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply multi-factor, multisignature approvals, and timelock mechanisms for all administrative changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policies, multisig transaction records, and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validator operator keys
checklist_items.description: Adopt hardware security modules, multi-party computation, and strict operational procedures to secure validator private keys against theft and compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely and are not compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware wallets, enforce strict access controls, and conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audits, HSM deployment logs, and security training records.

checklist_items.title: Monitor network and node infrastructure for DoS attack patterns
checklist_items.description: Implement network monitoring, anomaly detection, and DDoS mitigation strategies to protect validator nodes and infrastructure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators maintain operational security and network resilience.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use DDoS mitigation services, firewalls, and monitor for unusual traffic patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network monitoring dashboards, incident logs, and mitigation reports.

checklist_items.title: Apply rigorous input validation and signature verification in reward configuration updates
checklist_items.description: Ensure that reward configuration transactions are validated for correctness and signed by authorized personnel to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward configuration data is validated by the reward distribution system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict validation logic and require signatures from recognized keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validation logs, signature verification reports, and on-chain transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive auditing and formal verification of reward distribution logic
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit and formally verify the correctness of reward distribution algorithms to prevent exploitation and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward distribution logic is complex and critical to system fairness.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage third-party auditors, perform formal verification, and conduct testing for all reward-related code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and test case outcomes.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure all proposals and votes are validated through cryptographic signatures to prevent spoofing of $BGT Holders' identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The signature verification mechanism within the Governance System has a flaw or is improperly implemented.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions, and conduct regular security audits of the verification process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of signature validation processes and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Apply strong key management and access controls to validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Ensure validator private keys are securely stored, managed, and rotated, to prevent unauthorized signing of blocks and proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices, including hardware security modules and multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management procedures and access logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and monitoring for validator node availability
checklist_items.description: Implement procedures to ensure validator nodes remain operational and accessible, including network security, redundancy, and DoS mitigation measures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators are responsible for maintaining uptime and security of their own node infrastructure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply network security best practices, monitoring, and slashing mechanisms for downtime.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational reports, monitoring dashboards, and incident logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all smart contracts undergo comprehensive security audits and formal verification for critical logic
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit and verify the security properties of all smart contracts involved in governance, staking, reward, and bridging functionalities to detect and fix vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BEX smart contracts contain vulnerabilities exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform third-party audits, code reviews, and formal verification of critical smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, and code review logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and session management for BeraHub and related web interfaces
checklist_items.description: Strengthen user authentication workflows to prevent session hijacking, phishing, or unauthorized access to governance and staking actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend or its underlying smart contracts have vulnerabilities that allow for spoofing or malicious modifications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-factor authentication, session timeout, and security best practices in web app development.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, security configuration documentation, and penetration testing reports.

checklist_items.title: Configure and enforce strict access controls and permission checks on Reward Vaults and incentive management contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure only authorized entities can modify incentive parameters, deposit or withdraw funds, and perform administrative operations in Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contracts have potential vulnerabilities or malicious logic allowing unauthorized tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control, multisig controls, and timelocks for critical functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code, access control role assignments, and transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and anti-DoS protections on web interfaces and smart contract functions vulnerable to flooding
checklist_items.description: Prevent attackers from overwhelming the system with high volumes of requests, such as claim submissions, proposals, or staking actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system lacks sufficient protection against request flooding that could impact availability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and transaction fee mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Traffic logs, system capacity reports, and DDoS mitigation plans.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses and user interactions during bridging and staking transactions to prevent spoofing and tampering
checklist_items.description: Use verified contract addresses, enable transaction details validation, and educate users to prevent malicious transactions resulting from phishing or interface spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users are vulnerable to visiting phishing sites or interacting with unverified contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use trusted links, verify contract addresses via official channels, and educate users on scam avoidance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, verification protocols, and public communications.

checklist_items.title: Enforce inclusion of all critical transaction parameters in signatures for bridging, redemption, and staking actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users sign transactions that include all necessary parameters (recipient address, amount, nonce) to prevent tampering or Man-in-the-Middle alterations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Transaction parameters can be manipulated if not included in the signed payload.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use standards like EIP-712 for structured data signing and ensure wallet UI displays all relevant parameters clearly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction data, code snippets, and wallet interface screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust monitoring and anomaly detection for smart contract admin functions and reward configuration changes
checklist_items.description: Detect and alert on suspicious or unauthorized modifications to critical configuration parameters in Reward Vaults and incentive contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault admin actions may be malicious or accidental, affecting system integrity.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy logging, real-time monitoring, and alerting systems for administrative transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, alert reports, and incident response procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust multisig key management practices for Guardian multisig wallets
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Guardian multisig-wallet private keys are securely stored, regularly audited, and managed with best practices to prevent control compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating secure key management policies and multisig wallet audits.

checklist_items.title: Establish transaction parameter verification and non-malleability measures in Guardian multisig operations
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig members to meticulously verify all transaction parameters, and employ non-malleable transaction structures to prevent tampering before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Policy documentation and process audit logs showing transaction verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Ensure off-chain governance system smart contracts are thoroughly audited and incorporate security best practices
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits of governance smart contracts, especially those handling proposal cancellations, to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System might allow the Guardian's cancellation transaction to have unintended broader effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, access control, and business logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report demonstrating vulnerability remediation.

checklist_items.title: Implement smart contract access control mechanisms with least privilege principles for Governance and Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure all critical functions within governance and Reward Vault smart contracts are protected by strict access controls, possibly with multisig authorization and role-based permissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Governance System might allow the Guardian's cancellation action to have unintended broader effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) and multisignature protections for sensitive functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code and deployment configuration showing access controls.

checklist_items.title: Verify the integrity and authenticity of the Airdrop Claim Application frontend
checklist_items.description: Use DNSSEC, HTTPS, and code integrity mechanisms to ensure users access the legitimate Airdrop Claim Application, and educate users on verifying official URLs and certificates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports of verified URLs and HTTPS certificates; URL whitelists.
checklist_items.owner: Community Outreach and Security Awareness Team

checklist_items.title: Enforce signed transaction verification including all critical parameters in the Airdrop Claim process
checklist_items.description: Ensure all critical claim parameters (recipient address, amount, nonce) are included in an EIP-712 signature from the user's wallet, and verify signatures on-chain before processing claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure, and the transaction parameters can be tampered before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters are part of the signed message. Use HTTPS for frontend. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audits of signing procedures; on-chain verification logs.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract and Frontend Security Teams

checklist_items.title: Implement strong cryptographic signature validation for governance actions
checklist_items.description: Enforce strict on-chain signature verification for all governance proposals and votes to prevent spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can exploit a vulnerability in the user's wallet or the signing process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic signature verification and key management workflows.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review documentation and signature validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Restrict proposal and vote transaction submission to non-spam by implementing rate limits and transaction costs
checklist_items.description: Configure governance smart contracts to prevent spamming by setting minimum fees or proposal thresholds, and implement rate-limiting measures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System contracts can be overwhelmed by a high volume of net transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement gas cost limits, proposal thresholds, and anti-spam mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code; deployment parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement timely slashing penalties for malicious or inactive validators
checklist_items.description: Configure the network to detect and penalize validators that do not propose blocks or attempt to spam/attack the consensus, including slashing or removal.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The network has mechanisms to detect and penalize malicious validator behavior.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement slashing conditions for inactivity or misbehavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validator performance logs; slashing transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware security modules (HSMs) and secure key management for Validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Store validator private keys in HSMs or secure enclaves, and rotate keys regularly to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key storage solutions and key rotation policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management system audit logs; security certification documents.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust key management and regular audits for the Guardian multisig wallet.
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig wallet private keys are securely stored, managed, and regularly audited to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports or key management procedures documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce meticulous verification of all transaction parameters in multisig approval workflows.
checklist_items.description: Require guardians to verify all transaction details, especially cancellation targets, before signing, to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction records with verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities and implement best security practices.
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of governance-related smart contracts to identify and mitigate vulnerabilities such as reentrancy, access control flaws, or logic errors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System might allow the Guardian's cancellation action to have unintended, broader effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, bug bounty reports, or formal verification documents.

checklist_items.title: Secure social login mechanisms and link social accounts to verified wallet addresses with secondary verification.
checklist_items.description: Implement strong secondary verification and security measures for social login-based claim authentication to prevent impersonation through compromised social accounts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Social login is an accepted method for authenticating airdrop claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Prioritize wallet-based claims with signature verification. If social login is used, enforce strong 2FA and securely link social accounts to verified wallet addresses. Implement fraud detection for suspicious claim patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs showing linked social accounts and secondary authentication methods.

checklist_items.title: Enhance user education and implement domain verification and anti-phishing measures for the Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.description: Educate users and employ technical safeguards such as domain verification, HTTPS, and anti-phishing warnings to prevent interaction with fraudulent sites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, phishing site block lists, or domain verification records.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication channels and ensure all claim parameters are included in cryptographically signed messages.
checklist_items.description: Design the claim process so that all critical parameters (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the user's wallet-signed message, preventing tampering during transmission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure (e.g., no HTTPS or compromised local network).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed messages with all claim parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and infrastructure protections against request flooding and DDoS attacks.
checklist_items.description: Deploy rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions, use CAPTCHAs where applicable, and employ network-level DDoS protections to secure the Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks. Employ Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS protection services for the frontend. Optimize smart contract gas usage to make abuse costly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs showing request rates and mitigation effectiveness.

checklist_items.title: Audit smart contracts for access control vulnerabilities and implement least privilege principles.
checklist_items.description: Perform security audits focusing on access control mechanisms, ensuring only authorized entities can execute admin functions in Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) has administrative functions to manage incentives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct rigorous security audits focusing on access control. Use well-tested libraries for ownership/role management (e.g., OpenZeppelin). Minimize the attack surface of admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, access control matrix, verified upgrade and role assignment logs.

checklist_items.title: Develop and enforce robust private key management protocols for validator operators.
checklist_items.description: Operators should use hardware security modules (HSMs), multisig, and cold storage to prevent key compromise, with regular key rotation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices for validators. Monitor for anomalous staking activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet enrollment records, audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement smart contract upgrade mechanisms with timelocks and multisig approval.
checklist_items.description: Design Reward Vault and other critical contracts to support controlled upgrades, with timelocks and multisignature governance to prevent unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) smart contract contains a bug in its incentive management functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough security audits of Reward Vault smart contracts. Implement gas limits and checks against reentrancy or unbounded loops. Upgradability mechanism for bug fixes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Upgrade governance procedures, contract implementation with upgrade mechanisms, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Guardian multisig cancel proposals are verified through robust signature checks to prevent spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and signature verification measures for multisig controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs demonstrating signature validation procedures and successful multi-party verification.

checklist_items.title: Enforce transaction parameter verification and non-malleability in multisig proposals
checklist_items.description: Design Guardian multisig transaction processes such that all parameters are meticulously verified and non-malleable to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Signatures are validated, but transaction parameters could be tampered if not verified comprehensively.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish strict transaction parameter verification procedures among multisig signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Record of transaction parameter verification steps and approval logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust on-chain smart contract audits and formal verification for Governance System contracts
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough audits and formal verification of the governance smart contracts to find and fix vulnerabilities that could lead to privilege escalation or unauthorized control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contract vulnerabilities could allow a vulnerability in the governance system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular security audits, formal verification, and adherence to best practices in smart contract development.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification documentation, and code review records.

checklist_items.title: Secure wallet private key management and enforce hardware wallet usage
checklist_items.description: Ensure users manage private keys securely, use hardware wallets, and avoid sharing seed phrases to prevent private key disclosure vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User is responsible for protecting their wallet private keys/seed phrases.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallets, educate about secure key management, and implement wallet security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet setup procedures, user training materials, and hardware wallet adoption metrics.

checklist_items.title: Verify all critical transaction parameters are included and signed in the wallet's signed message (e.g., EIP-712 signatures)
checklist_items.description: Require all important parameters of airdrop claims and bridge transactions to be part of the cryptographic signature to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Final signed message from the user's wallet does not explicitly include all critical claim parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement explicit inclusion of all critical transaction parameters in the signature process, following standards like EIP-712.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed messages demonstrating proper inclusion of parameters and verification process.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and resource-based protections for the Airdrop Claim Application endpoints
checklist_items.description: Protect against high-volume request flooding to prevent denial of service in the token claiming process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: No effective rate-limiting or anti-DoS protections are currently in place.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limiting, anti-bot measures, and scalable backend infrastructure.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs showing request rates and mitigation effectiveness diagrams.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures, backups, and diversity among Guardian multisig signers
checklist_items.description: Develop operational security measures such as key backups, designated backup signers, and diverse geographical locations for multisig control to prevent unavailability of the Guardian multisig.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Fewer than 5 Guardian multisig signers may be unavailable, leading to failure to cancel malicious proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement procedural safeguards, backup signers, and operational readiness plans.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedure documentation, backup key logs, and training records.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance system smart contracts for reentrancy, access control, and privilege escalation vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive audits and testing of the Governance System's smart contracts to prevent privilege escalation and ensure proper access controls are enforced.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance smart contracts may have vulnerabilities allowing unintended privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular smart contract audits, use of secure development practices, and formal verification where feasible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification logs, and testing records.

checklist_items.title: Design and enforce smart contract access control mechanisms with least privilege principles and conduct regular security audits
checklist_items.description: Ensure Reward Vaults' smart contracts implement strict access control (e.g., onlyOwner) and undergo regular security audits to prevent unauthorized administrative actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults have admin functions protected by access control that could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement minimal privilege access controls, audit and verify access mechanisms regularly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, audit certifications, and access logs.

checklist_items.title: Use official verified interfaces and educate users to verify contract addresses before interacting with bridges
checklist_items.description: Prevent spoofing attacks by ensuring that users access only official bridge interfaces and verify contract addresses through trusted channels before bridging assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User may interact with malicious replicas of the bridge interface.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Distribute official URLs, use domain monitoring, and conduct user awareness campaigns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs of user communication channels, outreach materials.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure transaction data structure for asset bridging, excluding sensitive information to prevent data leakage
checklist_items.description: Design the bridge transaction protocol to only include necessary, non-sensitive data to prevent sensitive information from being publicly disclosed on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data structure for bridging transactions may include optional fields with sensitive info.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Review and restrict the payload to non-sensitive, essential details only.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Schema documentation, privacy review reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.description: Ensure all multisig cancellation transactions are verified against cryptographic signatures from the multisig wallet to prevent impersonation or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature verification for multisig transactions.

checklist_items.title: Require all Guardian multisig signers to meticulously verify transaction parameters before signing.
checklist_items.description: Establish a formal process or mechanism that mandates each multisig signer to verify all transaction details, especially proposal cancellation targets, prior to signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A compromised or malicious Guardian signer could attempt to tamper with the details of a cancellation transaction they are co-signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Confirmation logs or signed statements from signers verifying transaction details.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong access control and comprehensive security audits for Governance System smart contracts.
checklist_items.description: Require thorough security audits and formal verification of all governance-related smart contracts, focusing on proposal creation and voting mechanisms, to detect vulnerabilities that may enable privilege escalation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System allows a transaction signed by the Guardian (intended for cancelling a proposal) to perform actions beyond its defined scope.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, access control, and other common attack vectors. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing absence of critical vulnerabilities; formal verification reports if applicable.

checklist_items.title: Verify the authenticity of the Airdrop Claim Application domain and use official channels for claim interactions.
checklist_items.description: Educate users to interact only with verified, official URLs and ensure browser indicators (e.g., HTTPS, trusted certificate) are used to confirm the legitimacy of the Airdrop Claim Application interface.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of secure connections; user reports of phishing attempts.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure communication protocols and monitor for front-end compromises in the Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.description: Implement HTTPS, Subresource Integrity, and regular frontend security audits to prevent modifications or injections in (or on) the Airdrop Claim Application frontend that could lead to tampering or data manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application endpoint allows querying by arbitrary wallet addresses or social identifiers without strong authentication or rate limiting.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication for eligibility checks. Only return eligibility status (and amount if applicable) for the authenticated user. Apply rate limiting and CAPTCHAs to eligibility check endpoints to prevent mass querying. Avoid using easily guessable or enumerable identifiers in URLs or API calls related to allocation data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security configurations; logs showing enforcement of HTTPS, SRI, and rate limiting.

checklist_items.title: Establish robust rate limiting and anti-DoS protections on the Airdrop Claim Application backend.
checklist_items.description: Implement rate limiting on all API endpoints and smart contract interactions related to claiming or eligibility checking, and deploy anti-DDoS measures such as firewalls and monitoring, to prevent request flooding and service disruption.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks. Employ Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS protection services for the frontend.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Rate limiting logs; incident reports of request flooding.

checklist_items.title: Apply comprehensive security audits and formal verification to BEX smart contracts involved in asset management.
checklist_items.description: Conduct third-party audits and formal verification of the BEX contracts to identify and eliminate vulnerabilities that could allow asset tampering or unauthorized transfers during deposit, withdrawal, or swap operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BEX smart contracts (104) contain a bug (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect calculation, oracle manipulation if applicable).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous smart contract audits, formal verification, secure oracle integration, comprehensive testing of all modes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports; formal verification reports; test suite results.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict input validation and rejection of malformed reward configurations in the Distributor (116) and L1 reward logic.
checklist_items.description: Enforce validation of reward distribution parameters, such as vault addresses and weights, within reward configuration transactions to prevent malformed or malicious data from affecting PoL reward distribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The L1 (Component 101) correctly calculates and attributes all forms of validator rewards. The Distributor component (116), which receives DF 1006, which is a trusted part of or interacts closely with L1 (101) PoL mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor contract (116) and any L1 (101) logic that processes this data. Reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validation logs; test cases demonstrating rejection of invalid configs.

checklist_items.title: Audit L1 (Component 101) reward logic and enforce strict checks to prevent over- or under-claiming of validator rewards.
checklist_items.description: Conduct an audit and implement safeguards for the reward calculation and attribution logic to ensure validators cannot claim more than entitled, preventing exploits that manipulate reward attribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The L1 (Component 101) correctly calculates and attributes all forms of validator rewards (fees, $BGT, incentives via integrated components).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough auditing and formal verification of all smart contracts and L1 modules involved in reward calculation and distribution. Implement strong access controls and invariant checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports; formal verification certificates; monitoring logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust multisig key management and regular audits for Guardian multisig wallets
checklist_items.description: Mitigate spoofing threats by securing Guardian multisig keys and conducting regular vulnerability assessments to prevent unauthorized cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing multi-signature implementation and recent security assessments.

checklist_items.title: Enforce meticulous parameter verification before multisig signature approval for proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require Guardian multisig members to verify all transaction parameters, especially proposal targets, before signing to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of verification steps taken by each Guardian signatory.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures including key backups and diversity among Guardian signers
checklist_items.description: Develop and enforce operational protocols to ensure Guardian authorization remains available, even during unforeseen events, to prevent DDoS-related failure to cancel malicious proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Operational failures, such as key loss or unavailability of signers, can prevent Guardian multisig from acting.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures for Guardian members, including key backup and recovery plans, clear communication protocols, and designated backup signers if feasible. Ensure geographical and operational diversity among signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of operational procedures, backup plans, and training records.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities and apply best practices for security and access control
checklist_items.description: Mitigate risks of privilege escalation and privilege abuse by conducting comprehensive security audits focusing on access control and vulnerability assessments.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Governance System might allow the Guardian's cancellation action to have unintended, broader effects.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, access control, and known attack vectors. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports with vulnerability assessments and remediation steps.

checklist_items.title: Implement enforcement of signed transaction inclusion of all critical parameters using standards like EIP-712
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering by ensuring all transaction-critical data (proposal, target proposal ID, parameters) are included and verified in the cryptographic signature process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: All critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim are part of the EIP-712 signed message. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed messages and proof of inclusion of all critical parameters.

checklist_items.title: Verify the authenticity of bridge interface URLs and contract addresses via official sources
checklist_items.description: Reduce risks of spoofing attacks by guiding users to access legitimate bridge interfaces and verifying smart contract addresses before interacting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User 7 might click on a phishing link leading to a fake bridge interface.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official and verified interfaces for bridge interaction. Users should bookmark correct addresses and verify contract addresses through trusted sources.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, URL verification logs, educational material distribution records.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all bridge transaction data, especially recipient address, is included in the signed message for end-to-end integrity
checklist_items.description: Prevent Man-in-the-Middle tampering by enforcing signing of all critical transaction parameters, including recipient address, to detect and reject altered transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure (e.g., no HTTPS or compromised local network).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters are included in the signed message. Use HTTPS for the frontend and secure wallet signing procedures. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample transaction payloads demonstrating inclusion of critical parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and anti-DDoS protections on Airdrop Claim Application endpoints
checklist_items.description: Mitigate DoS risks by deploying traffic filtering, rate limiting, and user detection mechanisms to prevent flooding or resource exhaustion.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement throttling, CAPTCHAs, and network security measures for the application's infrastructure.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs demonstrating rate limiting and traffic patterns.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic signatures and whitelist safeguards for reward delegation transactions
checklist_items.description: Prevent impersonation by requiring strict signature verification and maintaining whitelisted accounts, reducing spoofing and impersonation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The $BGT Holder (Actor 4) could have their $BGT delegated without their consent if their account or session is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use cryptographic signatures for delegation transactions; implement whitelists and session security in BeraHub.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample delegation transactions, whitelist records, and signature validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify the integrity and authenticity of delegation parameters in the frontend interface before signing
checklist_items.description: Mitigate tampering by validating delegation transaction contents, such as validator address and delegation amount, before user approval.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BeraHub frontend can be compromised to display false information or alter transaction details.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement zero-knowledge proofs or signatures over contract parameters, display confirmation in hardware wallets, educate users to verify details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction details, audit logs of frontend validation steps.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access control and validation logic in Reward Vaults to prevent unauthorized asset management
checklist_items.description: Mitigate privilege escalation by auditing access control checks and ensuring only authorized accounts can modify incentive parameters or withdraw assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) contains a vulnerability allowing unauthorized actions due to flawed access control.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Review and enforce access control permissions, use multisig for admin operations, and require owner approval for sensitive changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control implementation documentation, audit logs of administrative actions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure multisig key management practices prevent unauthorized Guardian access
checklist_items.description: Implement robust key management, regular audits, and secure storage for Guardian multisig keys to mitigate the risk of impersonation and unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multisig key access and management procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction parameter verification protocols for multisig cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig signers to meticulously verify transaction details before signing, possibly through secure communication channels and non-malleable transaction parameters, to prevent tampering and misdirection of proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Procedures and logs demonstrating verification steps taken by signers.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures and backups for Guardian multisig availability
checklist_items.description: Develop robust operational procedures, including key backups, failover signers, and operational diversity among Guardian members, to ensure timely cancellation of malicious proposals even during outages or unavailability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A malicious proposal is in its timelock period and requires cancellation. Fewer than 5 Guardian multisig members are available, willing, or able to sign the cancellation transaction within the timelock period.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures for Guardian members, including key backup and recovery plans, clear communication protocols, and designated backup signers if feasible. Ensure geographical and operational diversity among signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedures and backup plans documented and tested.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance smart contracts for vulnerabilities related to privilege escalation and unintended control
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of Governance System smart contracts to identify and remediate potential vulnerabilities that could allow elevation of privilege or unintended effects during proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's legitimate cancellation capability, could inadvertently grant the Guardian (or the transaction itself) unintended powers over the system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on reentrancy, incorrect access control, and other common vulnerabilities. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report confirming vulnerabilities are addressed.

checklist_items.title: Secure social login mechanisms and enforce secondary verification for Airdrop claim authentication
checklist_items.description: Use strong, multi-factor authentication and link social logins securely to wallet addresses. Prioritize wallet-based claims with message signatures and implement fraud detection to prevent impersonation of social login accounts for Airdrop claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Social login is an accepted method for authenticating airdrop claims. The user's social media account has been compromised. The Airdrop Claim Application does not implement sufficient secondary verification for claims made via social login.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Prioritize wallet-based claims with signature verification. If social login is used, enforce strong 2FA and securely link social accounts to verified wallet addresses. Implement fraud detection for suspicious claim patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multi-factor authentication and signature verification measures.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong user verification for front-end interactions with the Airdrop Claim Application
checklist_items.description: Verify that the frontend for the Airdrop Claim Application is official, using secure connection protocols, domain monitoring, and user education to prevent approached-based phishing and spoofing of the claiming interface.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs, use DNSSEC and HTTPS, educate users on phishing risks, and deploy anti-phishing measures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports of phishing awareness and verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Enforce end-to-end cryptographic signing of all critical Airdrop claim parameters
checklist_items.description: Ensure all claim transaction parameters, including recipient address, amount, and nonce, are included within a securely signed message (e.g., EIP-712) from the user's wallet to prevent tampering during transit or frontend compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's connection to the Airdrop Claim Application frontend is not secure (e.g., no HTTPS or compromised local network). The frontend of the Airdrop Claim Application itself is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message payloads and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure eligibility check APIs to prevent data leakage and enumeration attacks
