checklist_items.description: Implement strong authentication, return only minimal necessary information, and apply rate limiting and CAPTCHAs to prevent probing of allocation data that could compromise user privacy or enable targeted attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The eligibility check endpoint of the Airdrop Claim Application allows querying by arbitrary wallet addresses or social identifiers without strong authentication or rate limiting. The response from the eligibility check leaks more information than necessary.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication for eligibility checks. Only return eligibility status (and amount if applicable) for the authenticated user. Apply rate limiting and CAPTCHAs to eligibility check endpoints to prevent mass querying. Avoid using easily guessable or enumerable identifiers in URLs or API calls related to allocation data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: API logs showing rate limits and access controls.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and anti-DoS measures on the Airdrop Claim Application endpoints and smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Deploy rate limiting, request throttling, and monitoring to prevent flooding of eligibility checks and claim requests, ensuring service availability for legitimate users during high load or attack attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Airdrop Claim Application's frontend servers or backend smart contracts have limited capacity to handle requests. There are no effective rate-limiting mechanisms or bot protections in place for the eligibility check or claim functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks. Employ Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS protection services for the frontend. Optimize smart contract gas usage to make abuse costly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Rate limiting logs, WAF reports.

checklist_items.title: Deploy multisig control and timelocks for critical Reward Vault administration actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure reward management operations are protected by multisig and subject to timelocks, reducing the risk of malicious or accidental tampering with incentive parameters or token distributions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) has admin functions to manage incentives. These functions are intended to be protected by an access control mechanism (e.g., only callable by the Protocol Admin/Token Manager). A vulnerability exists in this access control mechanism.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement timelocks for significant rate changes. Use multisig for admin controls. Regular audits of admin activity and configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract implementation with multisig and timelock parameters documented.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all governance actions are authenticated via digital signatures from the proposer's private key to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System's signature verification mechanism has a flaw.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions, secure key management by users, regular audits of Governance System contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed proposal or vote showing cryptographic validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Audit and verify access control logic of Reward Vault smart contracts before deployment
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough security audits focused on access control mechanisms to ensure only authorized entities can modify incentive rates or withdraw collateral.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the Rewards Vault's access control logic could allow an unauthorized actor to execute admin functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits focusing on access control. Use well-tested libraries for ownership/role management (e.g., OpenZeppelin). Minimize the attack surface of admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report confirming access control mechanisms and no backdoors.

checklist_items.title: Require multisignature approval for critical reward configuration changes
checklist_items.description: Implement multisig approval for all modifications to reward distribution parameters, such as incentive rates, to prevent unilateral malicious or accidental changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault could have an upgrade mechanism or administrative functions that, if compromised or misused, allow for tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement timelocks for significant rate changes. Use multisig for admin controls. Regular audits of admin activity and configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig approval records and change logs for reward configurations.

checklist_items.title: Validate all input parameters in reward allocation transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all reward configuration data submitted to the blockchain are validated for correctness and consistency before acceptance.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system can handle or reject invalid configurations; current validation may be insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor contract and any L1 logic that processes this data. Reject invalid configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test cases demonstrating rejection of malformed or invalid reward configurations.

checklist_items.title: Ensure secure management of validator private keys
checklist_items.description: Utilize hardware wallets, multisig, and hardware security modules to protect the private keys used for proposing and validating blocks, preventing impersonation and unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices for validators. Monitor for anomalous staking activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key storage and access control, hardware wallet certification documents.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict transaction validation and monitoring for validator actions
checklist_items.description: Implement comprehensive validation, monitoring, and anomaly detection for all validator-initiated transactions to detect and prevent unusual or malicious proposals and attestations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Berachain L1 relies on cryptographic signatures to authenticate staking transactions; validation processes are in place.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous validation, monitor for anomalous activities, and establish alerting for suspicious validator behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs with validation outcome reports, incident alerts for suspected malicious activity.

checklist_items.title: Deploy anti-spoofing measures for user-facing bridge and dApp interfaces
checklist_items.description: Use official, verified URLs, implement browser security headers, and educate users to prevent phishing and spoofing attacks on bridge and dApp websites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User relies on external, potentially untrustworthy, sources to find the bridge or dApp interface.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official and verified interfaces, implement security headers, and conduct user security awareness campaigns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, phishing site takedown records, browser security test results.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction signing safeguards to prevent tampering and reward misattribution
checklist_items.description: Use hardware wallets, ensure all transaction details are clearly presented, and verify transaction parameters before signing to prevent tampering and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Wallet software can alter or misrepresent transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use reputable wallets, hardware security modules, and verify transaction details meticulously.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs, user confirmation records.

checklist_items.title: Establish comprehensive smart contract testing and formal verification processes
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit, test, and formally verify smart contracts involved in reward distribution, staking, and bridging to detect and fix vulnerabilities proactively.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts might contain bugs or backdoors that can be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security audits, formal verification, and include bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, bug bounty findings.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and anomaly detection for reward, staking, and bridging activities
checklist_items.description: Establish real-time monitoring and alerting systems for suspicious or abnormal transactions in reward, staking, and bridging contracts to enable rapid response to potential exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system can perform effective monitoring and anomaly detection.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Set up monitoring dashboards, alerting systems, and incident response procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring system logs, alert records, incident reports.

checklist_items.title: Enforce multi-factor authentication and secure session management for governance platform access
checklist_items.description: Require MFA, strong passwords, and secure session controls to prevent session hijacking and unauthorized governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker gains control of user account or session to spoof governance actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement MFA, session timeout, and IP address/session tracking.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Authentication logs, session management logs.

checklist_items.title: Use private mempools or onion routing techniques to hide transaction contents during proposal creation
checklist_items.description: Implement privacy-preserving methods like private mempools or threshold encryption to mitigate MEV and information disclosure risks in block proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Block proposal contents may be disclosed before consensus, enabling MEV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt privacy-enhancing technologies in consensus layer or proposal broadcasting.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Analysis reports demonstrating privacy measures, metrics on information leakage.

checklist_items.title: Regularly review and test validator consensus participation infrastructure for resilience against DoS attacks
checklist_items.description: Implement operational security best practices, infrastructure redundancy, and monitoring to minimize the risk and impact of DoS attacks on validator nodes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator nodes are responsible for maintaining uptime; they're targets for DoS.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy redundant infrastructure, DDoS mitigation, and continuous monitoring.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Uptime logs, DDoS mitigation reports, incident response records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict verification and validation procedures for all validator operator keys and hardware security modules
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all keys used for block proposal and signing are stored securely, with multi-factor authorization and hardware security modules where applicable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, enforce multi-factor authorization, and conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Hardware security module audit reports, key access logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce multi-signature approval workflows for critical smart contract operations related to reward and incentive mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Require multiple authorized signatures for functions that modify reward parameters or transfer large amounts of assets, reducing risk of single-point compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward modification functions can be misused if not requiring multisig.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig workflows, timelocks for admin actions, and activity audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig approval records, timelock schedules, operation logs.

checklist_items.title: Develop a formal incident response plan for potential exploits in reward, staking, and bridging contracts
checklist_items.description: Prepare incident response procedures and teams trained to rapidly respond to exploit detections in smart contracts or operational infrastructure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system is vulnerable to exploits in reward, staking, or bridging contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish incident response procedures, escalation paths, and regular simulation exercises.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Incident response plan documents, simulation exercise reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig transactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Guardian multisig cancellation transactions are verified for valid signatures to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust multisig signature verification, use multisig smart contracts, and perform regular audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs and multisig audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures for Guardian multisig key management and regular audits
checklist_items.description: Develop and enforce operational best practices for the secure management of Guardian multisig private keys, including regular key audits, diversified key holdings, and contingency planning.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig relies on cryptographic identities that can be compromised if keys are poorly managed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party key management, regular key rotations, and independent audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, key management policies.

checklist_items.title: Configure smart contracts to enforce strict validation of proposal parameters and transaction data
checklist_items.description: Add on-chain validation logic to reject any proposal cancellation transaction with tampered or inconsistent parameters before execution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System might allow a transaction with tampered parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Embed parameter validation logic within the multisig smart contract; use non-malleable identifiers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code audits, transaction validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Use formal verification and security audits for Reward Vault smart contracts, focusing on access control and reward distribution logic
checklist_items.description: Conduct rigorous smart contract audits using formal verification tools where feasible, and incorporate bug bounty programs to identify vulnerabilities before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) smart contract contains a bug in its incentive management functions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough security audits of Reward Vault smart contracts. Implement gas limits and checks against reentrancy or unbounded loops. Upgradability mechanism for bug fixes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, bug bounty findings.

checklist_items.title: Develop and deploy smart contracts with proper access controls to prevent unauthorized privilege escalation in Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Use established libraries and best practices (e.g., OpenZeppelin Ownable, Role-based access control) to ensure only authorized roles can modify critical parameters or withdraw assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) has administrative functions to manage incentives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits focusing on access control. Use well-tested libraries for ownership/role management (e.g., OpenZeppelin). Minimize the attack surface of admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and access control configuration documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust multisig key management practices for Guardian multisig wallet.
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Guardian multisig signing keys are managed securely to prevent compromise that could lead to spoofing and tampering threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports on multisig key management procedures and security measures.

checklist_items.title: Establish transaction verification procedures for Guardian multisig to prevent parameter tampering.
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig members to meticulously verify transaction parameters (e.g., proposal ID) before signing, to prevent tampering and ensure integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating verification steps during multisig signing.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities that could allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits of the Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control and privilege escalation vectors to prevent unauthorized power elevation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's transaction, could inadvertently grant unintended powers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, results of formal verification tests, and security assessments.

checklist_items.title: Secure social login mechanisms and link social accounts to verified wallets with secondary verification.
checklist_items.description: Enhance authentication for airdrop claims by linking social accounts to verified wallets with secondary verification steps, reducing the risk of account compromise and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's social media account has been compromised, enabling impersonation in claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Prioritize wallet-based claims with signature verification. Enforce strong 2FA and securely link social accounts to verified wallet addresses. Implement fraud detection for suspicious claim patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs showing secondary authentication steps, linkage procedures, and fraud detection reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify transaction signatures to prevent impersonation and spoofing in airdrop claims.
checklist_items.description: Utilize signature verification protocols (e.g., EIP-712) to ensure all claim transactions are genuinely authorized by the user's private key, preventing spoofing and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The social login is used for claim authentication, which is vulnerable if not properly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message from the user's wallet. Use HTTPS for the frontend and Subresource Integrity (SRI) for scripts. Conduct regular frontend security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports demonstrating signature verification mechanisms, logs of signature validation checks.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and CAPTCHA to prevent DoS attacks on the Airdrop Claim Application endpoints.
checklist_items.description: Apply rate limiting and CAPTCHAs on the eligibility check and claim submission endpoints to prevent request flooding and denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The application lacks sufficient protection against request flooding, impacting its availability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting on API endpoints and smart contract functions. Use CAPTCHAs for eligibility checks. Employ Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS protection services for the frontend. Optimize smart contract gas usage to make abuse costly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation showing rate limits, CAPTCHA implementation logs.

checklist_items.title: Design and implement smart contract validation to prevent exploitation of reward calculation logic.
checklist_items.description: Apply formal verification and thorough audits to Reward Vault and related reward calculation contracts to prevent logic errors that could be exploited for unauthorized or incorrect rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contracts contain bugs (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect reward logic).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough smart contract audits, formal verification for critical logic, secure oracle integration if used for collateral pricing, comprehensive testing of all modes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, test case reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls and role management for Reward Vault administrative functions.
checklist_items.description: Apply robust access control mechanisms (e.g., OpenZeppelin's Ownable and Roles) to prevent unauthorized admin actions and privilege escalation in Reward Vault contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (Component 105) has administrative functions protected by an owner/manager role, which may have vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits focusing on access control. Use well-tested libraries for ownership/role management (e.g., OpenZeppelin). Minimize the attack surface of admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control role assignment and management logs, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish and enforce procedure for secure private key management for Validator keys.
checklist_items.description: Ensure validators use hardware wallets, multi-signature schemes, and secure backup procedures for private keys to prevent compromise and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator, but risks of compromise remain.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key management practices for validators. Monitor for anomalous staking activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet deployment records, audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Audit and test Berachain node infrastructure to mitigate DoS on consensus participation.
checklist_items.description: Implement infrastructure security best practices, DDoS protection, and redundant network configurations to ensure node availability for consensus participation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators are responsible for maintaining the uptime and security of their own node infrastructure, but risks of DoS remain.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Validators should implement robust operational security, including DoS protection for their nodes. The network should have mechanisms to penalize prolonged validator downtime (slashing).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, network monitoring logs.

checklist_items.title: Apply formal verification and security audits to validator proposer selection mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the proposer selection algorithm is correctly implemented and resistant to manipulation, using formal methods and auditing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposer selection algorithm in BeaconKit/CometBFT is correctly implemented and resistant to manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous auditing and formal verification (if possible) of the consensus mechanism's proposer selection logic. Continuous monitoring for deviations from expected proposer patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, monitoring logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all governance proposals and votes are cryptographically signed and signature verification is enforced on-chain.
checklist_items.description: Require all governance-related transactions to include valid cryptographic signatures to prevent SPOOFING and unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The $BGT Holder's identity can be spoofed if signature verification is flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandatory strong cryptographic signature verification for all governance actions, secure key management by users, regular audits of Governance System contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature validation logs, code review reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement anti-phishing measures and user education for bridge and dApp interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Educate users to verify official URLs, use official links, and recognize phishing signatures for the generic bridge and dApps to prevent SPOOFING and TAMpering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by phishing sites and malicious interfaces.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official bookmarks, wallet warnings for malicious sites, user education on phishing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security awareness training records, phishing test reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement cryptographic signature verification for Guardian multisig actions to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.description: Verify that multisig signatures are properly validated using cryptographic methods to ensure authenticity of cancellation requests.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System correctly validates signatures from the Guardian multisig address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust signature validation mechanisms and cryptographic checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of signature validation processes and cryptographic verification results.

checklist_items.title: Establish operational procedures for Guardian multisig members to ensure timely signatures and mitigate availability risks.
checklist_items.description: Create and enforce operational protocols, including key management, backup strategies, and communication channels, to prevent operational failures that can impair multisig functionality.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A malicious proposal is in its timelock period and requires cancellation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish robust operational procedures for Guardian members, including key backup and recovery plans.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of operational procedures, key management policies, and backup strategies.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits of Governance System smart contracts, focusing on vulnerabilities in proposal cancellation functions.
checklist_items.description: Perform thorough security assessments and auditing of smart contracts governing proposal cancellation to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could be exploited for elevation of privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect access control) exists in the Governance System's smart contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security audits by reputable third-party firms and implement secure coding practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports detailing findings and remediations.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict input validation for reward allocation configurations within the Distributor and L1 modules.
checklist_items.description: Implement robust validation checks to ensure reward allocation data is well-formed, within expected bounds, and free from malicious manipulations before acceptance and processing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system can validate reward configuration data for correctness and completeness.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation routines and reject malformed data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test results and validation logs demonstrating input validation effectiveness.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware wallets and multisignature schemes for validator key management to prevent theft and unauthorized signing.
checklist_items.description: Require validators to store private keys on hardware security modules or multisignature wallets, reducing the risk of key compromise that can lead to impersonation or malicious actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored securely by the validator operator.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware wallet support and multisig solutions for validator key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation, hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Configure reward distribution contracts with access controls and timelocks to prevent unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access controls, multi-factor approvals, and timelock mechanisms to ensure only authorized personnel can alter reward parameters.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault (105) has admin functions protected by an access control mechanism.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply RBAC, multisig authorities, and enforce timelock controls in smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control role assignments, deployment of multisig contracts, timelock configurations.

checklist_items.title: Educate users to verify contract addresses and domain ownership to prevent phishing attacks during asset transfer.
checklist_items.description: Provide guidance on how to distinguish official websites and contract addresses, and promote best practices for verifying transaction destinations before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User relies on external, potentially untrustworthy, sources to find the Reward Vault contract address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement user education campaigns, oficial verification protocols, and browser warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Educational material, phishing test results.

checklist_items.title: Require all critical transaction parameters to be included and signed via standardized schemes like EIP-712.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all important data fields in bridging and redemption transactions are cryptographically signed and included in the user's signed message, preventing tampering from off-chain or frontend components.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: All critical parameters of the claim (recipient address, amount, nonce) are part of the EIP-712 compliant signed message.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement structured signing schemes, enforce client-side signing of all critical parameters, and perform server-side verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audits and transaction verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and anti-DoS protections on frontend and smart contract interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Configure API endpoints, smart contract function calls, and node infrastructure with rate limits, CAPTCHAs, and other measures to mitigate flooding and resource exhaustion attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system lacks sufficient request limits or bot detection mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limiting middleware, CAPTCHA challenges, and network-level protections.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System configuration documentation, traffic logs showing rate-limiting enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and verify access controls related to token approvals in wallets.
checklist_items.description: Use tools like revoke.cash to review granted token permissions and educate users on revoking unnecessary approvals to prevent malicious contract interactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Wallet UI does not clearly and forcefully warn about the scope of permissions being granted.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage regular permission audits, integrate wallet tools that visualize approval scopes, and promote user education.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, user-reported approval statuses.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust key management and regular audits for Guardian multisig wallets.
checklist_items.description: Ensure multisig wallets used by Guardians are secured with best practices, including hardware security modules, multi-factor authentication, and regular code audits, to prevent compromise of guardian keys that can spoof privileged actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance System relies on the cryptographic identity of the Guardian multisig. If compromised, unauthorized cancellations can occur.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management and security practices for all Guardian multisig members. Regularly audit the multisig wallet contract for vulnerabilities. Consider diversity in signer setups.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, multisig wallet security audits, security policy documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce meticulous verification of transaction parameters within the Guardian multisig process.
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardian multisig signers to carefully verify all transaction details, especially the target proposal ID, before signing, to prevent tampering that could lead to incorrect proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker or malicious insider can modify the transaction data for a cancellation before it receives the required 5 of 9 signatures. Other signers might not meticulously verify all transaction parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all Guardian multisig members meticulously verify all parameters of a cancellation transaction before signing. Use secure communication channels for coordinating multisig actions. Design proposal identifiers to be non-malleable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of transaction verification steps, signed transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Audit Governance System smart contracts for vulnerabilities related to proposal cancellation functions.
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive security audits focused on proposal cancellation logic, including signature verification, access controls, and potential reentrancy vulnerabilities, to prevent privilege escalation threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Governance System, when triggered by the Guardian's legitimate cancellation capability, could inadvertently grant the Guardian (or the transaction itself) unintended powers over the system.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits of all Governance System smart contracts, focusing on access control, state changes, and interactions related to privileged functions. Apply the principle of least privilege strictly in contract design.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certifications, formal verification reports, code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Secure social login and account recovery mechanisms for airdrop claim application.
checklist_items.description: Implement strong secondary verification (e.g., wallet signature confirmation, two-factor authentication) for social login-based claim authentication, and educate users on the risks of social login compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Social login is an accepted method for authenticating airdrop claims. The user's social media account has been compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Prioritize wallet-based claims with signature verification. If social login is used, enforce strong 2FA and securely link social accounts to verified wallet addresses. Implement fraud detection for suspicious claim patterns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Account security policy documents, multi-factor authentication implementation logs.

checklist_items.title: Distribute official application URLs and domains through trusted channels.
checklist_items.description: Educate users to access the official Airdrop Claim Application via bookmarked links and verified domains, and implement domain monitoring and alerting for impersonation attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users can be deceived by sophisticated phishing websites mimicking the official Airdrop Claim Application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Clearly communicate official application URLs through multiple trusted channels. Use DNSSEC and HTTPS. Educate users on identifying phishing attempts. Consider anti-phishing measures like browser extensions or partnerships with security firms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User education materials, domain monitoring reports.
